NAME

protocol.h - Server packet processing and protocol implementation.

Detailed Description

Server packet processing and protocol implementation.

Definition in file src/server/protocol.h.

Function Documentation

void broadcast_clear_console_to_all_clients (void )

Signal all active clients to clear their displays before next video frame. Sets the needs_display_clear flag for all currently connected and active clients. This is used when the grid layout changes (clients join/leave) to ensure all clients clear their displays before receiving frames with the new layout.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses atomic flag (needs_display_clear) instead of packet queue
  • Send thread checks flag and sends CLEAR_CONSOLE before next video frame
  • Guarantees CLEAR_CONSOLE arrives before new grid layout frame

SYNCHRONIZATION:

  • Acquires g_client_manager_rwlock for reading
  • Uses atomic operations for flag setting (no mutex needed)
  • Thread-safe access to client list

USAGE SCENARIO:

  • Called when active video source count changes
  • Ensures all clients clear before new grid layout is sent
  • Prevents visual artifacts from old content

Note

This function iterates all clients but only flags active ones

Non-blocking - just sets atomic flags

Definition at line 1938 of file server/protocol.c.

1938                                                   {
1939   SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "broadcast_clear_console_to_all_clients() called - unexpected usage");
1940   log_warn("CLEAR_CONSOLE is now sent from render threads, not broadcast");
1941 }

    SET_ERRNO.






void
  disconnect_client_for_bad_data (client_info_t * client, const char *
  format, ...)


Definition at line 155 of file
  server/protocol.c.


155                                                                                     {
156   if (!client) {
157     return;
158   }
159 
160   protocol_cleanup_thread_locals();
161 
162   // Atomically set the disconnect flag and get the old value
163   // This ensures only one thread proceeds to disconnect (the first one)
164   bool already_requested = atomic_exchange_bool(&client->protocol_disconnect_requested, true);
165   if (already_requested) {
166     return;  // Already disconnecting, don't do it again
167   }
168 
169   char reason[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL] = {0};
170   if (format) {
171     va_list args;
172     va_start(args, format);
173     safe_vsnprintf(reason, sizeof(reason), format, args);
174     va_end(args);
175   } else {
176     SAFE_STRNCPY(reason, "Protocol violation", sizeof(reason));
177   }
178 
179   const char *reason_str = reason[0] != '\0' ? reason : "Protocol violation";
180   const char *client_id = client->client_id;
181 
182   socket_t socket_snapshot = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
183   const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = NULL;
184   acip_transport_t *transport_snapshot = NULL;
185 
186   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
187   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
188     socket_snapshot = client->socket;
189     if (client->crypto_initialized) {
190       crypto_ctx = crypto_handshake_get_context(&client->crypto_handshake_ctx);
191     }
192   }
193   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
194 
195   // Get transport reference for WebSocket clients
196   mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
197   transport_snapshot = client->transport;
198   mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
199 
200   // NOTE: Disconnecting a client due to the client's own bad behavior isn't an
201   // error for us, it's desired behavior for us, so we simply warn and do not
202   // have a need for asciichat_errno here.
203   log_warn("Disconnecting client %u due to protocol violation: %s", client_id, reason_str);
204 
205   if (socket_snapshot != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
206     // Protect socket writes with send_mutex to prevent race with send_thread.
207     // This receive_thread and send_thread both write to same socket.
208     mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
209 
210     asciichat_error_t log_result =
211         log_network_message(socket_snapshot, (const struct crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx, LOG_ERROR,
212                             REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_SERVER_TO_CLIENT, "Protocol violation: %s", reason_str);
213     if (log_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
214       log_warn("Failed to send remote log to client %u: %s", client_id, asciichat_error_string(log_result));
215     }
216 
217     asciichat_error_t send_result = packet_send_error(socket_snapshot, crypto_ctx, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, reason_str);
218     if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
219       log_warn("Failed to send error packet to client %u: %s", client_id, asciichat_error_string(send_result));
220     }
221 
222     mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
223   } else if (transport_snapshot) {
224     // For WebSocket clients, try to send error via transport
225     log_debug("Sending error to WebSocket client %u via transport", client_id);
226     acip_send_error(transport_snapshot, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, reason_str);
227   }
228 
229   APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
230   platform_sleep_ms(500);
231 
232   log_debug("Setting active=false in disconnect_client_for_bad_data (client_id=%u, reason=%s)", client_id, reason_str);
233   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
234   atomic_store_bool(&client->shutting_down, true);
235   atomic_store_bool(&client->send_thread_running, false);
236   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
237   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
238 
239   if (client->audio_queue) {
240     packet_queue_stop(client->audio_queue);
241   }
242 
243   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
244   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
245     socket_shutdown(client->socket, 2);
246     socket_close(client->socket);
247     client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
248   }
249   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
250 }










void
  handle_audio_batch_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data,
  size_t len)


Process AUDIO_BATCH packet - store efficiently batched audio
    samples. Handles the optimized audio packet format that bundles multiple
    sample chunks into a single packet. This reduces packet overhead and
    improves network efficiency for audio streaming.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
audio_batch_packet_t header:





  
 •

  
uint32_t batch_count: Number of sample chunks in this batch

  
 •

  
uint32_t total_samples: Total number of float samples

  
 •

  
uint32_t sample_rate: Samples per second (typically 44100)

  
 •

  
uint32_t channels: Number of audio channels (1 = mono)





  
•

  
float samples[total_samples]: IEEE 754 sample data




PERFORMANCE ADVANTAGES:


    
  
  • Reduces packet count by 5-10x compared to single audio packets
    • 
  
  • Lower network overhead and CPU context switching
    • 
  
  • Better burst tolerance with larger buffers
    • 



VALIDATION PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Header size matches audio_batch_packet_t
    • 
  
  • Total packet size matches header + samples
    • 
  
  • Sample count is within reasonable bounds
    • 
  
  • Client is authorized to send audio
    • 



BUFFER MANAGEMENT:


    
  
  • Extracts samples from packet payload
    • 
  
  • Stores in client->incoming_audio_buffer (same as single format)
    • 
  
  • Ring buffer automatically handles overflow
    • 
  
  • mixer.c consumes batched samples identically
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Invalid batch headers are logged and packet dropped
    • 
  
  • Oversized batches are rejected (prevents DoS)
    • 
  
  • Buffer allocation failures are handled gracefully
    • 



Parameters


client Source client providing batched audio data


data Packet payload containing batch header and samples


len Total size of packet payload in bytes


Note


This is the preferred audio packet format



Batching is transparent to audio processing pipeline




See also


handle_audio_packet() For legacy single-batch
  format



AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES For maximum batch size constant




Definition at line 1194 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1194                                                                                     {
1195   // Log every audio batch packet reception
1196   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Received audio batch packet from client %u (len=%zu, is_sending_audio=%d)",
1197                   client->client_id, len, atomic_load_bool(&client->is_sending_audio));
1198 
1199   VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA(client, data, "AUDIO_BATCH");
1200   VALIDATE_MIN_SIZE(client, len, sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t), "AUDIO_BATCH");
1201   VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED(client, "AUDIO_BATCH");
1202 
1203   // Parse batch header using utility function
1204   audio_batch_info_t batch_info;
1205   asciichat_error_t parse_result = audio_parse_batch_header(data, len, &batch_info);
1206   if (parse_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1207     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Failed to parse audio batch header");
1208     return;
1209   }
1210 
1211   uint32_t packet_batch_count = batch_info.batch_count;
1212   uint32_t total_samples = batch_info.total_samples;
1213   uint32_t sample_rate = batch_info.sample_rate;
1214 
1215   (void)packet_batch_count;
1216   (void)sample_rate;
1217 
1218   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, packet_batch_count, "batch_count", "AUDIO_BATCH");
1219   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, total_samples, "total_samples", "AUDIO_BATCH");
1220 
1221   size_t samples_bytes = 0;
1222   if (safe_size_mul(total_samples, sizeof(uint32_t), &samples_bytes)) {
1223     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_BATCH sample size overflow (samples=%u)", total_samples);
1224     return;
1225   }
1226 
1227   size_t expected_size = sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t) + samples_bytes;
1228   if (len != expected_size) {
1229     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_BATCH length mismatch: got %zu expected %zu", len, expected_size);
1230     return;
1231   }
1232 
1233   // Bounds check to prevent integer overflow on allocation
1234   // Maximum allowed samples: AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES * 2 (2048 samples)
1235   // This prevents total_samples * sizeof(float) from exceeding 8KB
1236   const uint32_t MAX_AUDIO_SAMPLES = AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES * 2;
1237   if (total_samples > MAX_AUDIO_SAMPLES) {
1238     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_BATCH too many samples: %u (max: %u)", total_samples,
1239                                    MAX_AUDIO_SAMPLES);
1240     return;
1241   }
1242 
1243   const uint8_t *samples_ptr = (const uint8_t *)data + sizeof(audio_batch_packet_t);
1244 
1245   // Safe allocation: total_samples is bounded above, so multiplication won't overflow
1246   size_t alloc_size = (size_t)total_samples * sizeof(float);
1247   float *samples = SAFE_MALLOC(alloc_size, float *);
1248   if (!samples) {
1249     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for audio sample conversion");
1250     return;
1251   }
1252 
1253   // Use helper function to dequantize samples
1254   asciichat_error_t dq_result = audio_dequantize_samples(samples_ptr, total_samples, samples);
1255   if (dq_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1256     SAFE_FREE(samples);
1257     return;
1258   }
1259 
1260 #ifndef NDEBUG
1261   static int recv_count = 0;
1262   recv_count++;
1263   if (recv_count % 100 == 0) {
1264     uint32_t raw0 = bytes_read_u32_unaligned(samples_ptr + 0 * sizeof(uint32_t));
1265     uint32_t raw1 = bytes_read_u32_unaligned(samples_ptr + 1 * sizeof(uint32_t));
1266     uint32_t raw2 = bytes_read_u32_unaligned(samples_ptr + 2 * sizeof(uint32_t));
1267     int32_t scaled0 = (int32_t)NET_TO_HOST_U32(raw0);
1268     int32_t scaled1 = (int32_t)NET_TO_HOST_U32(raw1);
1269     int32_t scaled2 = (int32_t)NET_TO_HOST_U32(raw2);
1270     log_info("RECV: network[0]=0x%08x, network[1]=0x%08x, network[2]=0x%08x", raw0, raw1, raw2);
1271     log_info("RECV: scaled[0]=%d, scaled[1]=%d, scaled[2]=%d", scaled0, scaled1, scaled2);
1272     log_info("RECV: samples[0]=%.6f, samples[1]=%.6f, samples[2]=%.6f", samples[0], samples[1], samples[2]);
1273   }
1274 #endif
1275 
1276   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer) {
1277     asciichat_error_t write_result = audio_ring_buffer_write(client->incoming_audio_buffer, samples, total_samples);
1278     if (write_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1279       log_error("Failed to write decoded audio batch to buffer: %s", asciichat_error_string(write_result));
1280     }
1281   }
1282 
1283   SAFE_FREE(samples);
1284 }

    AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES, audio_dequantize_samples(),
    audio_parse_batch_header(), audio_ring_buffer_write(),
    audio_batch_info_t::batch_count, client_info::client_id,
    disconnect_client_for_bad_data(), ERROR_MEMORY,
    client_info::incoming_audio_buffer,
    client_info::is_sending_audio, log_debug_every,
    log_error, log_info, LOG_RATE_DEFAULT,

    safe_size_mul, audio_batch_info_t::sample_rate,
    SET_ERRNO, audio_batch_info_t::total_samples,
    VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED, VALIDATE_MIN_SIZE,
    VALIDATE_NONZERO, and VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA.






void
  handle_audio_opus_batch_packet (client_info_t * client, const void *
  data, size_t len)


Process AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH packet - efficient Opus-encoded audio
    batch from client. Handles batched Opus-encoded audio frames sent by the
    client. This provides ~98% bandwidth reduction compared to raw PCM audio
    while maintaining excellent audio quality.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
opus_batch_header_t (16 bytes):





  
 •

  
sample_rate (4 bytes)

  
 •

  
frame_duration (4 bytes)

  
 •

  
frame_count (4 bytes)

  
 •

  
reserved (4 bytes)





  
•

  
Opus encoded data (variable size, typically ~60 bytes/frame @ 24kbps)




PROCESSING FLOW:



  
1.

  
Parse batch header

  
2.

  
Decode each Opus frame back to PCM samples (960 samples/frame @
    48kHz)

  
3.

  
Write decoded samples to client's incoming audio buffer




PERFORMANCE:


    
  
  • Bandwidth: ~60 bytes/frame vs ~3840 bytes/frame for raw PCM (64:1
      compression)
    • 
  
  • Quality: Excellent at 24 kbps VOIP mode
    • 
  
  • Latency: 20ms frames for real-time audio
    • 



Parameters


client Client that sent the audio packet


data Packet payload data


len Packet payload length


See also


av_receive_audio_opus_batch() For packet parsing



opus_codec_decode() For Opus decoding




handle_audio_batch_packet() For raw PCM Audio batch
    handling




Definition at line 1321 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1321                                                                                          {
1322   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "Received Opus audio batch from client %u (len=%zu)", client->client_id, len);
1323 
1324   VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA(client, data, "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1325   VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED(client, "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1326   VALIDATE_RESOURCE_INITIALIZED(client, client->opus_decoder, "Opus decoder");
1327 
1328   // Parse Opus batch packet
1329   const uint8_t *opus_data = NULL;
1330   size_t opus_size = 0;
1331   const uint16_t *frame_sizes = NULL;
1332   int sample_rate = 0;
1333   int frame_duration = 0;
1334   int frame_count = 0;
1335 
1336   asciichat_error_t result = packet_parse_opus_batch(data, len, &opus_data, &opus_size, &frame_sizes, &sample_rate,
1337                                                      &frame_duration, &frame_count);
1338 
1339   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1340     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Failed to parse AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH packet");
1341     return;
1342   }
1343 
1344   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, frame_count, "frame_count", "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1345   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, opus_size, "opus_size", "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1346 
1347   // Calculate samples per frame (20ms @ 48kHz = 960 samples)
1348   int samples_per_frame = (sample_rate * frame_duration) / 1000;
1349   VALIDATE_RANGE(client, samples_per_frame, 1, 4096, "samples_per_frame", "AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH");
1350 
1351   // Use static buffer for common case to avoid malloc in hot path
1352   // Typical batches: 1-32 frames of 960 samples = up to 30,720 samples
1353   // Static buffer holds 32 frames @ 48kHz 20ms = 30,720 samples (120KB)
1354 #define OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES (32 * 960)
1355   static float static_decode_buffer[OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES];
1356 
1357   size_t total_samples = (size_t)samples_per_frame * (size_t)frame_count;
1358   float *decoded_samples;
1359   bool used_malloc = false;
1360 
1361   if (total_samples <= OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES) {
1362     decoded_samples = static_decode_buffer;
1363   } else {
1364     // Unusual large batch - fall back to malloc
1365     log_warn("Client %u: Large audio batch requires malloc (%zu samples)", client->client_id, total_samples);
1366     decoded_samples = SAFE_MALLOC(total_samples * sizeof(float), float *);
1367     if (!decoded_samples) {
1368       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer for Opus decoded samples");
1369       return;
1370     }
1371     used_malloc = true;
1372   }
1373 
1374   // Decode each Opus frame using frame_sizes array
1375   int total_decoded = 0;
1376   size_t opus_offset = 0;
1377 
1378   for (int i = 0; i < frame_count; i++) {
1379     // Get exact frame size from frame_sizes array (convert from network byte order)
1380     size_t frame_size = (size_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(frame_sizes[i]);
1381 
1382     // DEBUG: Log the actual bytes of each Opus frame
1383     if (frame_size > 0) {
1384       log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Client %u: Opus frame %d: size=%zu, first_bytes=[0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x]",
1385                       client->client_id, i, frame_size, opus_data[opus_offset] & 0xFF,
1386                       frame_size > 1 ? (opus_data[opus_offset + 1] & 0xFF) : 0,
1387                       frame_size > 2 ? (opus_data[opus_offset + 2] & 0xFF) : 0,
1388                       frame_size > 3 ? (opus_data[opus_offset + 3] & 0xFF) : 0);
1389     }
1390 
1391     if (opus_offset + frame_size > opus_size) {
1392       log_error("Client %u: Frame %d size overflow (offset=%zu, frame_size=%zu, total=%zu)", client->client_id, i + 1,
1393                 opus_offset, frame_size, opus_size);
1394       if (used_malloc) {
1395         SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1396       }
1397       return;
1398     }
1399 
1400     // SECURITY: Bounds check before writing decoded samples to prevent buffer overflow
1401     // An attacker could send malicious Opus frames that decode to more samples than expected
1402     if ((size_t)total_decoded + (size_t)samples_per_frame > total_samples) {
1403       log_error("Client %u: Opus decode would overflow buffer (decoded=%d, frame_samples=%d, max=%zu)",
1404                 client->client_id, total_decoded, samples_per_frame, total_samples);
1405       if (used_malloc) {
1406         SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1407       }
1408       return;
1409     }
1410 
1411     int decoded_count = opus_codec_decode((opus_codec_t *)client->opus_decoder, &opus_data[opus_offset], frame_size,
1412                                           &decoded_samples[total_decoded], samples_per_frame);
1413 
1414     if (decoded_count < 0) {
1415       log_error("Client %u: Opus decoding failed for frame %d/%d (size=%zu)", client->client_id, i + 1, frame_count,
1416                 frame_size);
1417       if (used_malloc) {
1418         SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1419       }
1420       return;
1421     }
1422 
1423     total_decoded += decoded_count;
1424     opus_offset += frame_size;
1425   }
1426 
1427   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Client %u: Decoded %d Opus frames -> %d samples", client->client_id, frame_count,
1428                   total_decoded);
1429 
1430   // DEBUG: Log sample values to detect all-zero issue
1431   static int server_decode_count = 0;
1432   server_decode_count++;
1433   if (total_decoded > 0 && (server_decode_count <= 10 || server_decode_count % 100 == 0)) {
1434     float peak = 0.0f, rms = 0.0f;
1435     for (int i = 0; i < total_decoded && i < 100; i++) {
1436       float abs_val = fabsf(decoded_samples[i]);
1437       if (abs_val > peak)
1438         peak = abs_val;
1439       rms += decoded_samples[i] * decoded_samples[i];
1440     }
1441     rms = sqrtf(rms / (total_decoded > 100 ? 100 : total_decoded));
1442     // Log first 4 bytes of Opus data to compare with client encode
1443     log_info("SERVER OPUS DECODE #%d from client %u: decoded_rms=%.6f, opus_first4=[0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x,0x%02x]",
1444              server_decode_count, client->client_id, rms, opus_size > 0 ? opus_data[0] : 0,
1445              opus_size > 1 ? opus_data[1] : 0, opus_size > 2 ? opus_data[2] : 0, opus_size > 3 ? opus_data[3] : 0);
1446   }
1447 
1448   // Write decoded samples to client's incoming audio buffer
1449   // Note: audio_ring_buffer_write returns error code, not sample count
1450   // Buffer overflow warnings are logged inside audio_ring_buffer_write if buffer is full
1451   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer && total_decoded > 0) {
1452     asciichat_error_t result = audio_ring_buffer_write(client->incoming_audio_buffer, decoded_samples, total_decoded);
1453     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1454       log_error("Client %u: Failed to write decoded audio to buffer: %d", client->client_id, result);
1455     }
1456   }
1457 
1458   if (used_malloc) {
1459     SAFE_FREE(decoded_samples);
1460   }
1461 }

    OPUS_DECODE_STATIC_MAX_SAMPLES, client_info::opus_decoder,

void
  handle_audio_opus_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data,
  size_t len)


Process AUDIO_OPUS packet - decode single Opus frame from client.
    Handles single Opus-encoded audio frames sent by clients. The packet format
    includes a 16-byte header followed by the Opus-encoded data.




PACKET STRUCTURE:


    
  
  • Offset 0: sample_rate (uint32_t, native byte order - bug in
    client)
    • 
  
  • Offset 4: frame_duration (uint32_t, native byte order - bug in
      client)
    • 
  
  • Offset 8: reserved (8 bytes)
    • 
  
  • Offset 16: Opus-encoded audio data
    • 



Parameters


client Client info structure


data Packet payload


len Packet length


Definition at line 1481 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1481                                                                                    {
1482   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, "Received Opus audio from client %u (len=%zu)", client->client_id, len);
1483 
1484   if (VALIDATE_PACKET_NOT_NULL(client, data, "AUDIO_OPUS")) {
1485     return;
1486   }
1487 
1488   // Minimum size: 16-byte header + at least 1 byte of Opus data
1489   if (len < 17) {
1490     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS packet too small: %zu bytes", len);
1491     return;
1492   }
1493 
1494   if (!atomic_load_bool(&client->is_sending_audio)) {
1495     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS received before audio stream enabled");
1496     return;
1497   }
1498 
1499   if (!client->opus_decoder) {
1500     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Opus decoder not initialized");
1501     return;
1502   }
1503 
1504   // Parse header (16 bytes) - convert from network byte order
1505   const uint8_t *buf = (const uint8_t *)data;
1506   uint32_t sample_rate_net, frame_duration_net;
1507   memcpy(&sample_rate_net, buf, 4);
1508   memcpy(&frame_duration_net, buf + 4, 4);
1509   uint32_t sample_rate = NET_TO_HOST_U32(sample_rate_net);
1510   uint32_t frame_duration = NET_TO_HOST_U32(frame_duration_net);
1511 
1512   // Extract Opus data (after 16-byte header)
1513   const uint8_t *opus_data = buf + 16;
1514   size_t opus_size = len - 16;
1515 
1516   // Validate parameters
1517   if (sample_rate == 0 || sample_rate > 192000) {
1518     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS invalid sample_rate: %u", sample_rate);
1519     return;
1520   }
1521 
1522   if (frame_duration == 0 || frame_duration > 120) {
1523     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS invalid frame_duration: %u ms", frame_duration);
1524     return;
1525   }
1526 
1527   // Calculate expected samples per frame
1528   int samples_per_frame = (int)((sample_rate * frame_duration) / 1000);
1529   if (samples_per_frame <= 0 || samples_per_frame > 5760) { // Max 120ms @ 48kHz
1530     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "AUDIO_OPUS invalid samples_per_frame: %d", samples_per_frame);
1531     return;
1532   }
1533 
1534   // Decode Opus frame
1535   float decoded_samples[5760]; // Max Opus frame size (120ms @ 48kHz)
1536   int decoded_count =
1537       opus_codec_decode((opus_codec_t *)client->opus_decoder, opus_data, opus_size, decoded_samples, samples_per_frame);
1538 
1539   if (decoded_count < 0) {
1540     log_error("Client %u: Opus decoding failed (size=%zu)", client->client_id, opus_size);
1541     return;
1542   }
1543 
1544   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_VERY_FAST, "Client %u: Decoded Opus frame -> %d samples", client->client_id, decoded_count);
1545 
1546   // Write decoded samples to client's incoming audio buffer
1547   if (client->incoming_audio_buffer && decoded_count > 0) {
1548     asciichat_error_t write_result =
1549         audio_ring_buffer_write(client->incoming_audio_buffer, decoded_samples, decoded_count);
1550     if (write_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1551       log_error("Failed to write decoded Opus samples to buffer: %s", asciichat_error_string(write_result));
1552     }
1553   }
1554 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    audio_ring_buffer_write(), client_info::client_id,
    disconnect_client_for_bad_data(),
    client_info::incoming_audio_buffer,
    client_info::is_sending_audio, log_debug_every,
    log_error, LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, LOG_RATE_VERY_FAST,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, opus_codec_decode(),
    client_info::opus_decoder, and VALIDATE_PACKET_NOT_NULL.






void
  handle_audio_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Process AUDIO packet - store single audio sample batch (legacy
    format). Handles the original audio packet format that sends one batch of
    float samples per packet. This format is less efficient than AUDIO_BATCH but
    still supported for backward compatibility.




PACKET STRUCTURE:


    
  
  • float samples[len/sizeof(float)] (IEEE 754 format)
    • 
  
  • Sample rate assumed to be 44100 Hz
    • 
  
  • Mono audio (single channel)
    • 



PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Less efficient than handle_audio_batch_packet()
    • 
  
  • Higher packet overhead per sample
    • 
  
  • Still real-time capable for typical loads
    • 



BUFFER MANAGEMENT:


    
  
  • Stores samples in client->incoming_audio_buffer (lock-free ring
    buffer)
    • 
  
  • Buffer overflow drops oldest samples to maintain real-time behavior
    • 
  
  • mixer.c consumes samples for multi-client audio mixing
    • 



STATE VALIDATION:


    
  
  • Only processes if client->is_sending_audio is true
    • 
  
  • Requires valid buffer pointer and non-zero length
    • 
  
  • Handles buffer pointer safely during shutdown
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Invalid packets are silently ignored
    • 
  
  • Buffer overflow is handled by ring buffer (drops old data)
    • 
  
  • Graceful shutdown behavior
    • 



Parameters


client Source client providing audio data


data Packet payload containing float audio samples


len Size of packet payload in bytes


Note


Prefer AUDIO_BATCH format for better efficiency



Audio processing happens in mixer threads, not here




See also


handle_audio_batch_packet() For efficient batched
  format



audio_ring_buffer_write() For storage implementation




Definition at line 1097 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1097                                                                               {
1098   VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA(client, data, "AUDIO");
1099   VALIDATE_AUDIO_ALIGNMENT(client, len, sizeof(float), "AUDIO");
1100   VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED(client, "AUDIO");
1101 
1102   int num_samples = (int)(len / sizeof(float));
1103   VALIDATE_AUDIO_SAMPLE_COUNT(client, num_samples, AUDIO_SAMPLES_PER_PACKET, "AUDIO");
1104   VALIDATE_RESOURCE_INITIALIZED(client, client->incoming_audio_buffer, "audio buffer");
1105 
1106   const float *samples = (const float *)data;
1107   asciichat_error_t result = audio_ring_buffer_write(client->incoming_audio_buffer, samples, num_samples);
1108   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1109     log_error("Failed to write audio samples to buffer: %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1110   }
1111 }

    AUDIO_SAMPLES_PER_PACKET, client_info::incoming_audio_buffer,
    log_error, VALIDATE_AUDIO_ALIGNMENT,
    VALIDATE_AUDIO_SAMPLE_COUNT, VALIDATE_AUDIO_STREAM_ENABLED,
    VALIDATE_NOTNULL_DATA, and VALIDATE_RESOURCE_INITIALIZED.






void
  handle_client_capabilities_packet (client_info_t * client, const void *
  data, size_t len)


Process CLIENT_CAPABILITIES packet - configure client-specific
    rendering. This packet contains detailed information about the client's
    terminal capabilities and preferences. The server uses this data to generate
    appropriately formatted ASCII art and ANSI escape sequences.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
terminal_capabilities_packet_t containing:





  
 •

  
width, height: Terminal dimensions in characters

  
 •

  
capabilities: Bitmask of terminal features

  
 •

  
color_level: ANSI color support level (1, 8, 16, 256, 24-bit)

  
 •

  
color_count: Number of supported colors

  
 •

  
render_mode: Foreground, background, or half-block rendering

  
 •

  
term_type: $TERM environment variable value

  
 •

  
colorterm: $COLORTERM environment variable value

  
 •

  
utf8_support: Whether terminal supports UTF-8

  
 •

  
palette_type: ASCII character palette preference

  
 •

  
palette_custom: Custom character set if PALETTE_CUSTOM




STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Updates client dimensions (width, height)
    • 
  
  • Stores complete terminal capabilities structure
    • 
  
  • Initializes per-client ASCII palette cache
    • 
  
  • Sets client->has_terminal_caps = true
    • 



PALETTE INITIALIZATION: The function performs critical palette
    setup:



  
1.

  
Determines character set based on palette_type

  
2.

  
Handles custom palettes if provided

  
3.

  
Generates luminance-to-character mapping

  
4.

  
Caches results for fast ASCII generation




THREAD SAFETY:


    
  
  • All client state updates are mutex-protected
    • 
  
  • Uses client->client_state_mutex for atomicity
    • 
  
  • Safe to call concurrently with render threads
    • 



VALIDATION PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Packet size matches expected structure
    • 
  
  • String fields are safely copied with bounds checking
    • 
  
  • Palette initialization is verified
    • 
  
  • Network byte order conversion
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Invalid packets are logged and ignored
    • 
  
  • Palette initialization failures use server defaults
    • 
  
  • Missing capabilities default to safe values
    • 



INTEGRATION IMPACT:


    
  
  • render.c uses capabilities for ASCII generation
    • 
  
  • Color output depends on client's color_level
    • 
  
  • Character selection uses initialized palette
    • 



Parameters


client Target client whose capabilities are being
  configured


data Packet payload containing terminal_capabilities_packet_t


len Size of packet payload in bytes


Note


This packet is typically sent once after CLIENT_JOIN



Capabilities can be updated during the session




Changes affect all subsequent ASCII frame generation




See also


initialize_client_palette() For Palette
  setup details



terminal_color_level_name() For color level
  descriptions

  server/protocol.c.

1677   VALIDATE_RANGE(client, caps->desired_fps, 1, 144, "desired_fps", "CLIENT_CAPABILITIES");

1692   client->terminal_caps.detection_reliable = caps->detection_reliable;

1694 
1695   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->terminal_caps.term_type, caps->term_type, sizeof(client->terminal_caps.term_type));
1696   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->terminal_caps.colorterm, caps->colorterm, sizeof(client->terminal_caps.colorterm));

1698   client->terminal_caps.utf8_support = NET_TO_HOST_U32(caps->utf8_support);

1700   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->terminal_caps.palette_custom, caps->palette_custom,

1702 
1703   client->terminal_caps.desired_fps = caps->desired_fps;
1704 
1705   // Extract wants_padding flag (1=padding enabled, 0=no padding for snapshot/piped modes)
1706   client->terminal_caps.wants_padding = (caps->wants_padding != 0);
1707 
1708   const char *custom_chars =

1727   client->codec_capabilities_audio = NET_TO_HOST_U32(caps->codec_capabilities_audio);

1729            client->codec_capabilities_video, client->codec_capabilities_audio);
1730 
1731   client->has_terminal_caps = true;
1732 
1733   log_info("Client %u capabilities: %ux%u, color_level=%s (%u colors), caps=0x%x, term=%s, colorterm=%s, "
1734            "render_mode=%s, reliable=%s, fps=%u, wants_padding=%d",
1735            client->client_id, client->width, client->height,
1736            terminal_color_level_name(client->terminal_caps.color_level), client->terminal_caps.color_count,
1737            client->terminal_caps.capabilities, client->terminal_caps.term_type, client->terminal_caps.colorterm,
1738            (client->terminal_caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK
1739                 ? "half-block"
1740                 : (client->terminal_caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND ? "background" : "foreground")),
1741            client->terminal_caps.detection_reliable ? "yes" : "no", client->terminal_caps.desired_fps,
1742            client->terminal_caps.wants_padding);
1743 
1744   // Send capabilities acknowledgment to client
1745   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1746     log_info_client(client, "Terminal configured: %ux%u, %s, %s mode, %u fps", client->width, client->height,
1747                     terminal_color_level_name(client->terminal_caps.color_level),
1748                     (client->terminal_caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK
1749                          ? "half-block"
1750                          : (client->terminal_caps.render_mode == RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND ? "background" : "foreground")),
1751                     client->terminal_caps.desired_fps);
1752   }
1753 
1754   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1755 
1756   log_warn("[CAPS_HANDLER] ✅ CAPS_COMPLETE: client_id=%u - all validations passed, capabilities stored",
1757            client->client_id);

void
  handle_client_join_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data,
  size_t len)


Process CLIENT_JOIN packet - client announces identity and
    capabilities. This is the first substantive packet clients send after
    establishing a TCP connection. It provides the server with essential
    information for managing the client throughout its session.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
client_info_packet_t containing:





  
 •

  
display_name: Human-readable client identifier

  
 •

  
capabilities: Bitmask of CLIENT_CAP_* flags




STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Updates client->display_name from packet
    • 
  
  • Sets client->can_send_video based on CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO
    • 
  
  • Sets client->can_send_audio based on CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO
    • 
  
  • Sets client->wants_stretch based on CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH
    • 



PROTOCOL BEHAVIOR:


    
  
  • Does NOT automatically start media streams (requires STREAM_START)
    • 
  
  • Does NOT send CLEAR_CONSOLE to other clients (prevents flicker)
    • 
  
  • Logs client capabilities for debugging
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Silently ignores packets with wrong size
    • 
  
  • Invalid display names are truncated safely
    • 
  
  • Missing capabilities default to false
    • 



Parameters


client Target client whose state will be updated


data Packet payload (should be client_info_packet_t)


len Size of packet payload in bytes


Note


This function should only be called by client receive
  threads



Client state is already protected by receive thread
  serialization




See also


handle_stream_start_packet() For enabling
  Media transmission


Definition at line 294 of file
  server/protocol.c.


294                                                                                     {
295   VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE(client, data, len, sizeof(client_info_packet_t), "CLIENT_JOIN");
296 
297   const client_info_packet_t *join_info = (const client_info_packet_t *)data;
298 
299   // Validate display name is present and not just whitespace
300   if (join_info->display_name[0] == '\0') {
301     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "CLIENT_JOIN display_name cannot be empty");
302     return;
303   }
304 
305   uint32_t capabilities = NET_TO_HOST_U32(join_info->capabilities);
306 
307   // Validate at least one capability flag is set
308   const uint32_t VALID_CAP_MASK = CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO | CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO | CLIENT_CAP_COLOR | CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH;
309   VALIDATE_CAPABILITY_FLAGS(client, capabilities, VALID_CAP_MASK, "CLIENT_JOIN");
310 
311   // Validate no unknown capability bits are set
312   VALIDATE_FLAGS_MASK(client, capabilities, VALID_CAP_MASK, "CLIENT_JOIN");
313 
314   SAFE_STRNCPY(client->display_name, join_info->display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN - 1);
315 
316   client->can_send_video = (capabilities & CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO) != 0;
317   client->can_send_audio = (capabilities & CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO) != 0;
318   client->wants_stretch = (capabilities & CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH) != 0;
319 
320   log_info("Client %u joined: %s (video=%d, audio=%d, stretch=%d)", client->client_id, client->display_name,
321            client->can_send_video, client->can_send_audio, client->wants_stretch);
322 
323   // Notify client of successful join (encrypted channel)
324   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
325     log_info_client(client, "Joined as '%s' (video=%s, audio=%s)", client->display_name,
326                     client->can_send_video ? "yes" : "no", client->can_send_audio ? "yes" : "no");
327   }
328 }






References client_info::can_send_audio,
    client_info::can_send_video,
    client_info_packet_t::capabilities, CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO,
    CLIENT_CAP_COLOR, CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH, CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO,
    client_info::client_id, disconnect_client_for_bad_data(),
    client_info::display_name, client_info_packet_t::display_name,
    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, log_info, log_info_client,
    MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN, NET_TO_HOST_U32, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    client_info::socket, VALIDATE_CAPABILITY_FLAGS,
    VALIDATE_FLAGS_MASK, VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE, and
    client_info::wants_stretch.






void
  handle_client_leave_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data,
  size_t len)


Process CLIENT_LEAVE packet - handle clean client disconnect.
    Clients may send this packet before disconnecting to allow the server to log
    the disconnect reason and perform clean state management. This is optional
    but preferred over abrupt disconnects.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
Optional string containing disconnect reason (0-256 bytes)




PROTOCOL BEHAVIOR:


    
  
  • Client logs the disconnect reason if provided
    • 
  
  • Server continues normal disconnect sequence after receiving packet
    • 
  
  • Client remains responsible for closing socket
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Empty payload is handled gracefully
    • 
  
  • Oversized payloads are rejected
    • 
  
  • Invalid UTF-8 in reason is handled gracefully (logged as-is)
    • 



Parameters


client Client that sent the leave packet


data Packet payload (optional reason string)


len Size of packet payload in bytes (0-256)


Note


This handler doesn't trigger immediate disconnect



Actual disconnect occurs when socket closes




Definition at line 437 of file
  server/protocol.c.


437                                                                                      {
438   if (!client) {
439     return;
440   }
441 
442   const char *client_id = client->client_id;
443 
444   if (len == 0) {
445     // Empty reason - client disconnecting without explanation
446     log_info("Client %u sent leave notification (no reason)", client_id);
447   } else if (len <= 256) {
448     // Reason provided - extract and log it
449     if (!data) {
450       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Client %u sent leave notification with non-zero length but NULL data", client_id);
451       return;
452     }
453 
454     char reason[257] = {0};
455     memcpy(reason, data, len);
456     reason[len] = '\0';
457 
458     // Validate reason is printable (handle potential non-UTF8 gracefully)
459     bool all_printable = true;
460     for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
461       uint8_t c = (uint8_t)reason[i];
462       if (c < 32 && c != '\t' && c != '\n') {
463         all_printable = false;
464         break;
465       }
466     }
467 
468     if (all_printable) {
469       log_info("Client %u sent leave notification: %s", client_id, reason);
470     } else {
471       log_info("Client %u sent leave notification (reason contains non-printable characters)", client_id);
472     }
473   } else {
474     // Oversized reason - shouldn't happen with validation.h checks
475     log_warn("Client %u sent oversized leave reason (%zu bytes, max 256)", client_id, len);
476   }
477 
478   // Deactivate client to stop processing packets
479   // Sets client->active = false immediately - triggers client cleanup procedures
480   log_debug("Setting active=false in handle_client_leave_packet (client_id=%u)", client_id);
481   atomic_store_bool(&client->active, false);
482 
483   // Note: We don't disconnect the client here - that happens when socket closes
484   // This is just a clean notification before disconnect
485 }






References client_info::active, atomic_store_bool(),
    client_info::client_id, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, log_debug,
    log_info, log_warn, and SET_ERRNO.






void
  handle_image_frame_h265_packet (client_info_t * client, const void *
  data, size_t len)


Process H.265-encoded image frame from client. Handles incoming
    H.265/HEVC-encoded video frames from clients. Frames are decoded to ASCII
    art and added to the compositing pipeline.




FLOW:



  
1.

  
Validate packet structure and minimum size

  
2.

  
Auto-enable video stream on first frame

  
3.

  
Decode H.265 packet to ASCII grid

  
4.

  
Convert ASCII to frame buffer format for rendering

  
5.

  
Store in client's incoming_video_buffer




Parameters


client Client connection info


data H.265 packet payload (includes header with frame dimensions)


len Payload size in bytes


PACKET FORMAT: [flags: u8][width: u16][height:
  u16][x265_data...]


    
  
  • flags: H265_FLAG_KEYFRAME, H265_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE
    • 
  
  • width/height: Frame dimensions in characters
    • 
  
  • x265_data: H.265-encoded frame data
    • 



Definition at line 939 of file
  server/protocol.c.


939                                                                                          {
940   // Validate codec capability for H.265 frames
941   if (!VIDEO_CODEC_SUPPORTED(client->codec_capabilities_video, VIDEO_CODEC_H265)) {
942     log_error("Client %s sent H.265 frame but does not support VIDEO_CODEC_H265 (capabilities=0x%x)",
943               client->client_id, client->codec_capabilities_video);
944     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "VIDEO_CODEC_H265 not supported by client (capabilities=0x%x)",
945                                    client->codec_capabilities_video);
946     return;
947   }
948 
949   if (!data || len < 5) {
950     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "H265_FRAME payload too small: %zu bytes (min 5)", len);
951     return;
952   }
953 
954   // Auto-enable video stream if not already enabled
955   // Use atomic_exchange to atomically set flag and check old value
956   bool was_sending_video = atomic_exchange_bool(&client->is_sending_video, true);
957   if (!was_sending_video) {
958     log_info("Client %s auto-enabled H.265 video stream (received IMAGE_FRAME_H265)", client->client_id);
959     if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
960       log_info_client(client, "First H.265 video frame received - streaming active");
961     }
962   }
963 
964   uint8_t flags = *(const uint8_t *)data;
965   bool is_keyframe = (flags & 0x01) != 0;
966   bool size_changed = (flags & 0x02) != 0;
967 
968   log_debug("H265_FRAME: flags=0x%02x, keyframe=%s, size_change=%s, len=%zu", flags, is_keyframe ? "yes" : "no",
969             size_changed ? "yes" : "no", len);
970 
971   if (!client->incoming_video_buffer) {
972     log_error("Client %s has no incoming video buffer for H.265 frames", client->client_id);
973     return;
974   }
975 
976   // Get H.265 server context (may be NULL during shutdown)
977   h265_server_context_t *h265_ctx = g_h265_server;
978   if (!h265_ctx) {
979     if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
980       log_error("H.265 codec server context not initialized");
981     }
982     return;
983   }
984 
985   // Get or create decoder for this client
986   uint32_t client_id_num = 0;
987   char *end = NULL;
988   unsigned long parsed = strtoul(client->client_id, &end, 10);
989   if (end != client->client_id && *end == '\0') {
990     client_id_num = (uint32_t)parsed;
991   } else {
992     // Fallback to string hash if can't parse as number
993     client_id_num = 0;
994     for (int i = 0; client->client_id[i]; i++) {
995       client_id_num = (client_id_num * 31 + (unsigned char)client->client_id[i]) & 0xFFFFFFFF;
996     }
997   }
998 
999   h265_server_client_t *client_decoder = h265_server_get_client_decoder(h265_ctx, client_id_num);
1000   if (!client_decoder) {
1001     log_error("Failed to get H.265 decoder for client %s", client->client_id);
1002     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Failed to initialize H.265 decoder");
1003     return;
1004   }
1005 
1006   // Allocate temporary RGBA buffer for decoding
1007   // H.265 frames from client are sent as [flags:u8][width:u16][height:u16][h265_data...]
1008   // Max frame is (256x256 = 65536 pixels) * 4 bytes/pixel = 262KB
1009   uint8_t *rgba_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(512 * 512 * 4, uint8_t *);
1010   if (!rgba_buf) {
1011     log_error("Failed to allocate RGBA buffer for H.265 decoding");
1012     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "Out of memory for H.265 frame decoding");
1013     return;
1014   }
1015 
1016   uint16_t frame_width = 0;
1017   uint16_t frame_height = 0;
1018   size_t rgba_size = 512 * 512 * 4;
1019 
1020   // Decode H.265 packet to RGBA
1021   asciichat_error_t decode_result = h265_server_decode_and_convert(client_decoder, (const uint8_t *)data, len, rgba_buf,
1022                                                                    &frame_width, &frame_height, &rgba_size);
1023 
1024   if (decode_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1025     log_error("H.265 decoding failed for client %s: %s", client->client_id, asciichat_error_string(decode_result));
1026     SAFE_FREE(rgba_buf);
1027     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "H.265 frame decode error");
1028     return;
1029   }
1030 
1031   // Write decoded frame to video buffer
1032   video_frame_t *frame = video_frame_begin_write(client->incoming_video_buffer);
1033   if (frame && frame->data) {
1034     memcpy(frame->data, rgba_buf, rgba_size);
1035     frame->size = rgba_size;
1036     frame->width = frame_width;
1037     frame->height = frame_height;
1038     frame->is_keyframe = is_keyframe;
1039 
1040     // Update frame metadata
1041     frame->receive_timestamp_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1042     frame->sequence_number = client->frames_received++;
1043 
1044     log_info_every(100, "RECV_H265 #%lu: client_id=%s size=%zu dims=%ux%u keyframe=%s", frame->sequence_number,
1045                    client->client_id, rgba_size, frame_width, frame_height, is_keyframe ? "yes" : "no");
1046 
1047     video_frame_commit(client->incoming_video_buffer);
1048   } else {
1049     log_warn("Failed to get write buffer for client %s (frame=%p, frame->data=%p)", client->client_id, (void *)frame,
1050              frame ? frame->data : NULL);
1051   }
1052 
1053   SAFE_FREE(rgba_buf);
1054 }

    client_info::codec_capabilities_video,
    disconnect_client_for_bad_data(), frame,
    client_info::frames_received, g_h265_server,
    g_should_exit, h265_server_decode_and_convert(),

void
  handle_image_frame_packet (client_info_t * client, void * data, size_t
  len)


Process IMAGE_FRAME packet - store client's video data for
    rendering. This is the most performance-critical packet handler, processing
    real-time video data from clients. It validates, stores, and tracks video
    frames for subsequent ASCII conversion and grid layout.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:


    
  
  • uint32_t width (network byte order)
    • 
  
  • uint32_t height (network byte order)
    • 
  
  • rgb_pixel_t pixels[width * height] (RGB888 format)
    • 



PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Called at 30fps per active client
    • 
  
  • Uses zero-copy storage when possible
    • 
  
  • Validates packet size before processing
    • 
  
  • Implements frame counting for debug logging
    • 



STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Auto-enables client->is_sending_video if not already set
    • 
  
  • Increments client->frames_received counter
    • 
  
  • Updates client dimensions if changed
    • 



BUFFER MANAGEMENT:


    
  
  • Stores entire packet (including dimensions) in
      client->incoming_video_buffer
    • 
  
  • Uses multi-frame ringbuffer for burst handling
    • 
  
  • Buffer overflow drops oldest frames (maintains real-time performance)
    • 
  
  • render.c threads consume frames for ASCII conversion
    • 



VALIDATION PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Packet size matches width * height * 3 + 8 bytes
    • 
  
  • Width and height are reasonable (prevents memory exhaustion)
    • 
  
  • Buffer pointers are valid before access
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Invalid packets are logged and dropped
    • 
  
  • Buffer overflow is handled gracefully
    • 
  
  • Shutdown conditions don't generate error spam
    • 



PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATIONS:


    
  
  • Debug logging is throttled (every 25000 frames)
    • 
  
  • Fast path for common case (valid packet with buffer space)
    • 
  
  • Minimal CPU work in receive thread (storage only)
    • 



Parameters


client Source client providing video data


data Packet payload containing image dimensions and RGB data


len Total size of packet payload in bytes


Note


This function is called by client receive threads at high
  frequency



Actual ASCII conversion happens in render threads (render.c)




Frame timestamps are added for synchronization purposes




See also


framebuffer_write_multi_frame() For buffer storage
  implementation



create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client() For frame
    consumption




Definition at line 737 of file
  server/protocol.c.


737                                                                               {
738   // Handle incoming image data from client
739   // New format: [width:4][height:4][compressed_flag:4][data_size:4][rgb_data:data_size]
740   // Old format: [width:4][height:4][rgb_data:w*h*3] (for backward compatibility)
741   // Use atomic compare-and-swap to avoid race condition - ensures thread-safe auto-enabling of video stream
742 
743   log_info("RECV_IMAGE_FRAME: client_id=%u, len=%zu", client->client_id, len);
744 
745   // Validate codec capability for RGBA frames
746   if (!VIDEO_CODEC_SUPPORTED(client->codec_capabilities_video, VIDEO_CODEC_RGBA)) {
747     log_error("Client %s sent RGBA frame but does not support VIDEO_CODEC_RGBA (capabilities=0x%x)",
748               client->client_id, client->codec_capabilities_video);
749     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "VIDEO_CODEC_RGBA not supported by client (capabilities=0x%x)",
750                                    client->codec_capabilities_video);
751     return;
752   }
753 
754   if (!data || len < sizeof(uint32_t) * 2) {
755     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME payload too small: %zu bytes", len);
756     return;
757   }
758   bool was_sending_video = atomic_load_bool(&client->is_sending_video);
759   if (!was_sending_video) {
760     // Try to atomically enable video sending
761     // Use atomic_compare_exchange_strong to avoid spurious failures
762     if (atomic_cas_bool(&client->is_sending_video, &was_sending_video, true)) {
763       log_info("Client %u auto-enabled video stream (received IMAGE_FRAME)", client->client_id);
764       // Notify client that their first video frame was received
765       if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
766         log_info_client(client, "First video frame received - streaming active");
767       }
768     }
769   } else {
770     // Log periodically to confirm we're receiving frames
771     // Use per-client counter protected by client_state_mutex to avoid race conditions
772     mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
773     client->frames_received_logged++;
774     if (client->frames_received_logged % 25000 == 0) {
775       char pretty[64];
776       format_bytes_pretty(len, pretty, sizeof(pretty));
777       log_debug("Client %u has sent %u IMAGE_FRAME packets (%s)", client->client_id, client->frames_received_logged,
778                 pretty);
779     }
780     mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
781   }
782 
783   // Parse image dimensions (use memcpy to avoid unaligned access)
784   uint32_t img_width_net, img_height_net;
785   memcpy(&img_width_net, data, sizeof(uint32_t));
786   memcpy(&img_height_net, (char *)data + sizeof(uint32_t), sizeof(uint32_t));
787   uint32_t img_width = NET_TO_HOST_U32(img_width_net);
788   uint32_t img_height = NET_TO_HOST_U32(img_height_net);
789 
790   log_debug("IMAGE_FRAME packet: width=%u, height=%u, payload_len=%zu", img_width, img_height, len);
791 
792   // Validate dimensions using image utility functions
793   if (image_validate_dimensions((size_t)img_width, (size_t)img_height) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
794     log_error("IMAGE_FRAME validation failed for dimensions: %u x %u", img_width, img_height);
795     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME invalid dimensions");
796     return;
797   }
798 
799   // Calculate RGB buffer size with overflow checking
800   size_t rgb_size = 0;
801   if (image_calc_rgb_size((size_t)img_width, (size_t)img_height, &rgb_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
802     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME buffer size calculation failed");
803     return;
804   }
805 
806   // Validate final buffer size against maximum
807   if (image_validate_buffer_size(rgb_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
808     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME buffer size exceeds maximum");
809     return;
810   }
811 
812   // Only support legacy format: [width:4][height:4][rgb_data:w*h*3]
813   if (rgb_size > SIZE_MAX - FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY) {
814     char size_str[32];
815     format_bytes_pretty(rgb_size, size_str, sizeof(size_str));
816     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME legacy packet size overflow: %s", size_str);
817     return;
818   }
819   size_t expected_size = FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY + rgb_size;
820 
821   if (len != expected_size) {
822     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME size mismatch: expected %zu bytes got %zu", expected_size, len);
823     return;
824   }
825 
826   // Validate legacy format
827   asciichat_error_t validate_result = frame_validate_legacy(len, rgb_size);
828   if (validate_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
829     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME legacy validation failed");
830     return;
831   }
832 
833   void *rgb_data = (char *)data + FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY;
834   size_t rgb_data_size = rgb_size;
835   bool needs_free = false;
836 
837   if (client->incoming_video_buffer) {
838     // Get the write buffer
839     video_frame_t *frame = video_frame_begin_write(client->incoming_video_buffer);
840 
841     if (frame && frame->data) {
842       // Build the packet in the old format for internal storage: [width:4][height:4][rgb_data:w*h*3]
843       // Use frame_check_size_overflow to validate overflow before repacking
844       asciichat_error_t overflow_check = frame_check_size_overflow(FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY, rgb_data_size);
845       if (overflow_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
846         if (needs_free && rgb_data) {
847           SAFE_FREE(rgb_data);
848         }
849         disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME size overflow while repacking");
850         return;
851       }
852       size_t old_packet_size = FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY + rgb_data_size;
853 
854       if (old_packet_size <= MAX_FRAME_BUFFER_SIZE) { // Max frame buffer size
855         uint32_t width_net = HOST_TO_NET_U32(img_width);
856         uint32_t height_net = HOST_TO_NET_U32(img_height);
857 
858         // Pack in old format for internal consistency
859         memcpy(frame->data, &width_net, sizeof(uint32_t));
860         memcpy((char *)frame->data + sizeof(uint32_t), &height_net, sizeof(uint32_t));
861         memcpy((char *)frame->data + sizeof(uint32_t) * 2, rgb_data, rgb_data_size);
862 
863         frame->size = old_packet_size;
864         frame->width = img_width;
865         frame->height = img_height;
866         frame->capture_timestamp_ns = (uint64_t)time(NULL) * NS_PER_SEC_INT;
867         frame->sequence_number = ++client->frames_received;
868 
869         // DEBUG: Compute hash of incoming RGB data to detect duplicates
870         uint32_t incoming_rgb_hash = 0;
871         for (size_t i = 0; i < rgb_data_size && i < 1000; i++) {
872           incoming_rgb_hash = (uint32_t)((uint64_t)incoming_rgb_hash * 31 + ((unsigned char *)rgb_data)[i]);
873         }
874 
875         // Per-client hash tracking (not static!) to avoid cross-client interference
876         const char *client_id = client->client_id;
877         bool is_new_frame = (incoming_rgb_hash != client->last_received_frame_hash);
878 
879         if (is_new_frame) {
880           log_info("RECV_FRAME #%u NEW: Client %u size=%zu dims=%ux%u hash=0x%08x (prev=0x%08x)",
881                    client->frames_received, client_id, rgb_data_size, img_width, img_height, incoming_rgb_hash,
882                    client->last_received_frame_hash);
883           client->last_received_frame_hash = incoming_rgb_hash;
884         } else {
885           log_info("RECV_FRAME #%u DUP: Client %u size=%zu dims=%ux%u hash=0x%08x", client->frames_received, client_id,
886                    rgb_data_size, img_width, img_height, incoming_rgb_hash);
887         }
888 
889         video_frame_commit(client->incoming_video_buffer);
890       } else {
891         if (needs_free && rgb_data) {
892           SAFE_FREE(rgb_data);
893         }
894         disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "IMAGE_FRAME repacked frame too large (%zu bytes)", old_packet_size);
895         return;
896       }
897     } else {
898       log_warn("Failed to get write buffer for client %u (frame=%p, frame->data=%p)", client->client_id, (void *)frame,
899                frame ? frame->data : NULL);
900     }
901   } else {
902     // During shutdown, this is expected - don't spam error logs
903     if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
904       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Client %u has no incoming video buffer!", client->client_id);
905     } else {
906       log_debug("Client %u: ignoring video packet during shutdown", client->client_id);
907     }
908   }
909 
910   // Clean up decompressed data if allocated
911   if (needs_free && rgb_data) {
912     SAFE_FREE(rgb_data);
913   }
914 }

    atomic_load_bool(), client_info::client_id,
    client_info::client_state_mutex,
    client_info::codec_capabilities_video,
    disconnect_client_for_bad_data(), ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    format_bytes_pretty(), frame,
    frame_check_size_overflow(), FRAME_HEADER_SIZE_LEGACY,
    frame_validate_legacy(), client_info::frames_received,
    client_info::frames_received_logged, g_should_exit,

    image_validate_buffer_size(), image_validate_dimensions(),
    client_info::incoming_video_buffer, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    client_info::is_sending_video,
    client_info::last_received_frame_hash, log_debug,

void
  handle_ping_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Handle PING packet - respond with PONG.


Definition at line 647 of file
  server/protocol.c.


647                                                                              {
648   (void)data;
649   (void)len;
650 
651   // Get transport reference briefly to avoid deadlock on TCP buffer full
652   mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
653   if (atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down) || !client->transport) {
654     mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
655     return;
656   }
657   acip_transport_t *pong_transport = client->transport;
658   mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
659 
660   // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex
661   asciichat_error_t pong_result = acip_send_pong(pong_transport);
662   if (pong_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
663     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send PONG response to client %u: %s", client->client_id,
664               asciichat_error_string(pong_result));
665   }
666 }

    atomic_load_bool(), client_info::client_id,
    ERROR_NETWORK, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    client_info::send_mutex, SET_ERRNO,
    client_info::shutting_down, and client_info::transport.

void
  handle_protocol_version_packet (client_info_t * client, const void *
  data, size_t len)


Process PROTOCOL_VERSION packet - validate protocol compatibility.
    Clients send this packet to announce their protocol version and
    capabilities. The server validates that the major version matches and logs
    any version mismatches for debugging purposes.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
protocol_version_packet_t containing:





  
 •

  
protocol_version: Major version number (must match
    PROTOCOL_VERSION_MAJOR)

  
 •

  
protocol_revision: Minor version number

  
 •

  
supports_encryption: Encryption capability flag

  
 •

  
compression_algorithms: Supported compression bitmask

  
 •

  
feature_flags: Optional feature flags




VALIDATION PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Packet size matches sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)
    • 
  
  • Major protocol version matches (PROTOCOL_VERSION_MAJOR)
    • 
  
  • Reserved bytes are zero (future-proofing)
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Version mismatch is logged but NOT fatal (backward compatibility)
    • 
  
  • Invalid packet size triggers disconnect
    • 



Parameters


client Client that sent the packet


data Packet payload (protocol_version_packet_t)


len Size of packet payload in bytes


Note


This is typically the first packet in the handshake



Protocol version validation ensures compatibility




Definition at line 361 of file
  server/protocol.c.


361                                                                                          {
362   if (!data) {
363     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "PROTOCOL_VERSION payload missing");
364     return;
365   }
366 
367   if (len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
368     disconnect_client_for_bad_data(client, "PROTOCOL_VERSION invalid size: %zu (expected %zu)", len,
369                                    sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
370     return;
371   }
372 
373   const protocol_version_packet_t *version = (const protocol_version_packet_t *)data;
374   uint16_t client_major = NET_TO_HOST_U16(version->protocol_version);
375   uint16_t client_minor = NET_TO_HOST_U16(version->protocol_revision);
376 
377   // Validate major version match (minor version can differ for backward compat)
378   if (client_major != PROTOCOL_VERSION_MAJOR) {
379     log_warn("Client %u protocol version mismatch: client=%u.%u, server=%u.%u", client->client_id, client_major,
380              client_minor, PROTOCOL_VERSION_MAJOR, PROTOCOL_VERSION_MINOR);
381     // Note: We don't disconnect on version mismatch for backward compatibility
382     // Clients may be older or newer than server
383   } else if (client_minor != PROTOCOL_VERSION_MINOR) {
384     log_info("Client %u has different protocol revision: client=%u.%u, server=%u.%u", client->client_id, client_major,
385              client_minor, PROTOCOL_VERSION_MAJOR, PROTOCOL_VERSION_MINOR);
386   }
387 
388   // Validate reserved bytes are zero
389   for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(version->reserved); i++) {
390     if (version->reserved[i] != 0) {
391       log_warn("Client %u sent non-zero reserved bytes in PROTOCOL_VERSION packet", client->client_id);
392       // Don't disconnect - reserved bytes may be used in future versions
393       break;
394     }
395   }
396 
397   // Log supported features
398   if (ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(version->supports_encryption)) {
399     log_debug("Client %u supports encryption", client->client_id);
400   }
401   if (version->compression_algorithms != 0) {
402     log_debug("Client %u supports compression: 0x%02x", client->client_id, version->compression_algorithms);
403   }
404   if (version->feature_flags != 0) {
405     uint16_t feature_flags = NET_TO_HOST_U16(version->feature_flags);
406     log_debug("Client %u supports features: 0x%04x", client->client_id, feature_flags);
407   }
408 }

    protocol_version_packet_t::reserved, and

void
  handle_size_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Process terminal size update packet - handle client window resize.
    Clients send this packet when their terminal window is resized, allowing the
    server to adjust ASCII frame dimensions accordingly. This ensures optimal
    use of the client's display area.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:



  
•

  
size_packet_t containing:





  
 •

  
uint16_t width: New terminal width in characters

  
 •

  
uint16_t height: New terminal height in characters




STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Updates client->width with new dimensions
    • 
  
  • Updates client->height with new dimensions
    • 
  
  • Thread-safe update using client state mutex
    • 



RENDERING IMPACT:


    
  
  • Subsequent ASCII frames will use new dimensions
    • 
  
  • Grid layout calculations will incorporate new size
    • 
  
  • No immediate frame regeneration (happens on next cycle)
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Invalid packet sizes are ignored silently
    • 
  
  • Extreme dimensions are accepted (client responsibility)
    • 
  
  • Concurrent updates are handled safely
    • 



Parameters


client Target client whose terminal was resized


data Packet payload containing new dimensions


len Size of packet payload (should be sizeof(size_packet_t))


Note


Changes take effect on the next rendering cycle



No validation of reasonable dimension ranges




Definition at line 1794 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1794                                                                              {
1795   VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE(client, data, len, sizeof(size_packet_t), "SIZE");
1796 
1797   const size_packet_t *size_pkt = (const size_packet_t *)data;
1798 
1799   // Extract and validate new dimensions
1800   uint16_t width = NET_TO_HOST_U16(size_pkt->width);
1801   uint16_t height = NET_TO_HOST_U16(size_pkt->height);
1802 
1803   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, width, "width", "SIZE");
1804   VALIDATE_NONZERO(client, height, "height", "SIZE");
1805   VALIDATE_RANGE(client, width, 1, 4096, "width", "SIZE");
1806   VALIDATE_RANGE(client, height, 1, 4096, "height", "SIZE");
1807 
1808   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1809   client->width = width;
1810   client->height = height;
1811   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
1812 
1813   log_info("Client %u updated terminal size: %ux%u", client->client_id, width, height);
1814 }

    VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE, VALIDATE_RANGE,
    client_info::width, and size_packet_t::width.






void
  handle_stream_start_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data,
  size_t len)


Process STREAM_START packet - client requests to begin media
    transmission. Clients send this packet to indicate they're ready to start
    sending video and/or audio data. The server updates its internal state to
    expect and process media packets from this client.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:


    
  
  • uint32_t stream_type (network byte order)
    • 
  
  • Bitmask containing STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO and/or STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO
    • 



STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • VIDEO: Records intention to send video (is_sending_video set by first
      IMAGE_FRAME)
    • 
  
  • AUDIO: Sets client->is_sending_audio = true if STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO
      present
    • 
  
  • Enables render threads to include this client in output generation
    • 



PROTOCOL BEHAVIOR:


    
  
  • Client must have announced capabilities via CLIENT_JOIN first
    • 
  
  • Server will start processing IMAGE_FRAME and AUDIO packets
    • 
  
  • Render threads will begin generating output for this client
    • 
  
  • Grid layout will be recalculated to include this client
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Ignores packets with incorrect size
    • 
  
  • Invalid stream types are silently ignored
    • 
  
  • Graceful handling if client lacks necessary capabilities
    • 



Parameters


client Target client starting media transmission


data Packet payload containing stream type flags


len Size of packet payload (should be sizeof(uint32_t))


Note


Changes take effect immediately for subsequent media
  packets



Render threads will detect the state change on their next
  cycle




See also


handle_stream_stop_packet() For stopping
  Media transmission



handle_image_frame_packet() For Video data
    processing




Definition at line 523 of file
  server/protocol.c.


523                                                                                      {
524   VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE(client, data, len, sizeof(uint32_t), "STREAM_START");
525 
526   uint32_t stream_type_net;
527   memcpy(&stream_type_net, data, sizeof(uint32_t));
528   uint32_t stream_type = NET_TO_HOST_U32(stream_type_net);
529 
530   // Validate at least one stream type flag is set
531   const uint32_t VALID_STREAM_MASK = STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO | STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO;
532   VALIDATE_CAPABILITY_FLAGS(client, stream_type, VALID_STREAM_MASK, "STREAM_START");
533 
534   // Validate no unknown stream type bits are set
535   VALIDATE_FLAGS_MASK(client, stream_type, VALID_STREAM_MASK, "STREAM_START");
536 
537   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) {
538     atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, true);
539   }
540   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) {
541     atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, true);
542 
543     // Create Opus decoder for this client if not already created
544     if (!client->opus_decoder) {
545       client->opus_decoder = opus_codec_create_decoder(48000);
546       if (client->opus_decoder) {
547         log_info("Client %u: Opus decoder created (48kHz)", client->client_id);
548       } else {
549         log_error("Client %u: Failed to create Opus decoder", client->client_id);
550       }
551     }
552   }
553 
554   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) {
555     log_info("Client %u announced video stream (waiting for first frame)", client->client_id);
556   }
557   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) {
558     log_info("Client %u started audio stream", client->client_id);
559   }
560 
561   // Notify client of stream start acknowledgment
562   const char *streams = (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) && (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO)
563                             ? "video+audio"
564                             : ((stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) ? "video" : "audio");
565   // Only send remote log to TCP clients (WebSocket clients have invalid socket)
566   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
567     log_info_client(client, "Stream started: %s", streams);
568   }
569 }






References atomic_store_bool(),
    client_info::client_id, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    client_info::is_sending_audio, client_info::is_sending_video,
    log_error, log_info, log_info_client,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, opus_codec_create_decoder(),
    client_info::opus_decoder, client_info::socket,
    STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO, STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO,
    VALIDATE_CAPABILITY_FLAGS, VALIDATE_FLAGS_MASK, and
    VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE.






void
  handle_stream_stop_packet (client_info_t * client, const void * data,
  size_t len)


Process STREAM_STOP packet - client requests to halt media
    transmission. Clients send this packet to gracefully stop sending video
    and/or audio data. The server updates its state to exclude this client from
    active media processing and grid layout calculations.




PACKET STRUCTURE EXPECTED:


    
  
  • uint32_t stream_type (network byte order)
    • 
  
  • Bitmask containing STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO and/or STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO
    • 



STATE CHANGES PERFORMED:


    
  
  • Sets client->is_sending_video = false if STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO present
    • 
  
  • Sets client->is_sending_audio = false if STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO present
    • 
  
  • Render threads will stop including this client in output
    • 



PROTOCOL BEHAVIOR:


    
  
  • Client remains connected but won't appear in video grid
    • 
  
  • Existing buffered media from this client will still be processed
    • 
  
  • Grid layout recalculates to exclude this client
    • 
  
  • Client can restart streaming with STREAM_START packet
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Ignores packets with incorrect size
    • 
  
  • Invalid stream types are silently ignored
    • 
  
  • Safe to call multiple times or when not streaming
    • 



Parameters


client Target client stopping media transmission


data Packet payload containing stream type flags


len Size of packet payload (should be sizeof(uint32_t))


Note


Changes take effect immediately



Render threads will detect the state change on their next
  cycle




See also


handle_stream_start_packet() For starting
  Media transmission


Definition at line 606 of file
  server/protocol.c.


606                                                                                     {
607   VALIDATE_PACKET_SIZE(client, data, len, sizeof(uint32_t), "STREAM_STOP");
608 
609   uint32_t stream_type_net;
610   memcpy(&stream_type_net, data, sizeof(uint32_t));
611   uint32_t stream_type = NET_TO_HOST_U32(stream_type_net);
612 
613   // Validate at least one stream type flag is set
614   const uint32_t VALID_STREAM_MASK = STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO | STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO;
615   VALIDATE_CAPABILITY_FLAGS(client, stream_type, VALID_STREAM_MASK, "STREAM_STOP");
616 
617   // Validate no unknown stream type bits are set
618   VALIDATE_FLAGS_MASK(client, stream_type, VALID_STREAM_MASK, "STREAM_STOP");
619 
620   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) {
621     atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_video, false);
622   }
623   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) {
624     atomic_store_bool(&client->is_sending_audio, false);
625   }
626 
627   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) {
628     log_info("Client %u stopped video stream", client->client_id);
629   }
630   if (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) {
631     log_info("Client %u stopped audio stream", client->client_id);
632   }
633 
634   // Notify client of stream stop acknowledgment
635   const char *streams = (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) && (stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO)
636                             ? "video+audio"
637                             : ((stream_type & STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO) ? "video" : "audio");
638   // Only send remote log to TCP clients (WebSocket clients have invalid socket)
639   if (client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
640     log_info_client(client, "Stream stopped: %s", streams);
641   }
642 }
