int
  send_server_state_to_client (client_info_t * client)


Send current server state to a specific client. Generates and
    queues a SERVER_STATE packet containing information about the current number
    of connected and active clients. This helps clients understand the
    multi-user environment and adjust their behavior accordingly.




PACKET CONTENT GENERATED:



  
•

  
server_state_packet_t containing:





  
 •

  
connected_client_count: Total clients connected to server

  
 •

  
active_client_count: Clients actively sending video/audio

  
 •

  
reserved: Padding for future extensions




USAGE SCENARIOS:


    
  
  • Initial state after client joins server
    • 
  
  • Periodic updates when client count changes
    • 
  
  • Response to client requests for server information
    • 



IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:


    
  
  • Counts active clients by scanning global client manager
    • 
  
  • Converts data to network byte order before queuing
    • 
  
  • Uses client's video queue for delivery
    • 
  
  • Non-blocking operation (queues for later delivery)
    • 



THREAD SAFETY:


    
  
  • Uses reader lock on global client manager
    • 
  
  • Safe to call from any thread
    • 
  
  • Atomic snapshot of client counts
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Returns -1 if client or queue is invalid
    • 
  
  • Queue overflow handled by packet_queue_enqueue()
    • 
  
  • No side effects on failure
    • 



Parameters


client Target client to receive server state
  information


Returns


0 on successful queuing, -1 on error


Note


Packet delivery happens asynchronously via send thread



Client count may change between queuing and delivery




See also


broadcast_server_state_to_all_clients() For
  multi-client updates


Definition at line 1868 of file
  server/protocol.c.


1868                                                        {
1869   if (!client) {
1870     return -1;
1871   }
1872 
1873   // Count active clients - LOCK OPTIMIZATION: Use atomic reads, no rwlock needed
1874   int active_count = 0;
1875   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1876     if (atomic_load_bool(&g_client_manager.clients[i].active)) {
1877       active_count++;
1878     }
1879   }
1880 
1881   // Prepare server state packet
1882   server_state_packet_t state;
1883   state.connected_client_count = active_count;
1884   state.active_client_count = active_count; // For now, all connected are active
1885   memset(state.reserved, 0, sizeof(state.reserved));
1886 
1887   // Convert to network byte order
1888   server_state_packet_t net_state;
1889   net_state.connected_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.connected_client_count);
1890   net_state.active_client_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(state.active_client_count);
1891   memset(net_state.reserved, 0, sizeof(net_state.reserved));
1892 
1893   // Send server state via ACIP transport
1894   // Protect socket writes with send_mutex to prevent race with send_thread.
1895   mutex_lock(&client->send_mutex);
1896   asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_server_state(client->transport, &net_state);
1897   mutex_unlock(&client->send_mutex);
1898 
1899   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1900     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send server state to client %u: %s", client->client_id,
1901               asciichat_error_string(result));
1902     return -1;
1903   }
1904 
1905   log_debug("Sent server state to client %u: %u connected, %u active", client->client_id, state.connected_client_count,
1906             state.active_client_count);
1907   return 0;
1908 }






References acip_send_server_state(),
    server_state_packet_t::active_client_count, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_load_bool(), client_info::client_id,
    server_state_packet_t::connected_client_count, ERROR_NETWORK,
    g_client_manager, HOST_TO_NET_U32, log_debug,
    MAX_CLIENTS, mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    server_state_packet_t::reserved, client_info::send_mutex,
    SET_ERRNO, and client_info::transport.


Referenced by add_webrtc_client().
